{"step_by_step_thinking": "Wilson disease is a rare genetic disorder that leads to copper accumulation in the liver and brain. The primary treatment for Wilson disease involves chelating agents that help remove excess copper from the body. Trientine, penicillamine, and zinc are commonly used chelating agents in Wilson disease. However, calcium is not a chelating agent and is not typically used in the treatment of Wilson disease.", "answer_choice": "B"}
